mechanism_narrative: Persistent near-zero federal funds rate compresses discount rates, raising equity valuations irrespective of short-term volatility. The accommodative stance signals continued policy support, driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative average 20-day forward returns over any 6-month period while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate from elevated post-crisis levels signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and earnings expectations, leading to positive SPX returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If UNRATE triggers a decline but forward 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than half of the triggered episodes over a 2-year walk-forward period, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with the federal funds rate anchored near zero produces deeply negative real interest rates. This encourages equity investment as a hedge against inflation, driving positive short-term returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%
regime_hint: low nominal rates with rising inflation
failure_condition: If the triggered periods show median SPX 20-day returns not significantly different from non-triggered periods (Wilcoxon test p > 0.05), reject interaction mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above the 600k threshold signal a durable recovery in residential investment. This regime shift boosts construction employment and consumer wealth via home equity, producing positive equity market returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If in the regime of HOUST > 600k, the S&P 500 fails to deliver a positive median 60-day forward return exceeding the unconditional median over a 5-year window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing and industrial output, a leading indicator for corporate earnings. Persistent positive growth in INDPRO signals a strengthening economy, elevating equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and subsequent 5-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (one-sided t-test, p < 0.10) over a rolling 3-year window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in monthly retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals sudden consumer spending weakness, which directly impacts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: If after an RSAFS decline trigger, the average 5-day SPX return is not negative (or is positive), the hypothesis is falsified over a 1-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market tightening. This joint signal strengthens the case for rising wage income and consumer spending, supporting equity prices over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: If the interaction variable (positive PAYEMS & declining UNRATE) does not produce a significantly higher median 20-day SPX return than either signal alone (tested via bootstrap, p > 0.05), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth above 3% annualized indicates robust economic momentum, which supports corporate profitability and investor confidence, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 3%
failure_condition: If in the regime of GDP growth > 3%, the median 20-day forward SPX return is not positive or is lower than the unconditional median over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.
